articles.title: STMicroelectronics Expands Silicon Carbide Production Capacity to Meet Growing EV Demand
articles.summary: STMicroelectronics announces significant expansion of SiC manufacturing capabilities with new production lines in Italy and Singapore to support the rapidly growing electric vehicle market.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics (ST) has announced a major expansion of its silicon carbide (SiC) manufacturing capabilities to meet the rapidly growing demand from the electric vehicle (EV) market. The company is investing $500 million to establish new production lines at its facilities in Catania, Italy and Singapore.

This expansion will increase ST's SiC wafer production capacity by 300% over the next three years. The new facilities will feature advanced 200mm SiC wafer processing capabilities, enabling higher manufacturing efficiency and cost reduction.

'We are seeing unprecedented demand for SiC devices from automotive customers,' said Jean-Marc Chery, President and CEO of STMicroelectronics. 'This investment positions us to support our customers' growth plans and maintain our leadership position in power electronics.'

The expansion includes:
- New 200mm SiC wafer fabrication lines
- Advanced packaging and testing capabilities
- Enhanced quality control systems for automotive-grade products
- Research and development facilities for next-generation SiC technologies

ST has already shipped over 1 billion SiC devices and supplies major automotive OEMs including Tesla, BMW, and Renault-Nissan-Mitsubishi. The company's SiC devices are used in EV traction inverters, onboard chargers, and DC-DC converters.

Production from the new lines is expected to begin in Q4 2026, with full capacity achieved by mid-2027.

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Launches STM32U5 Series with Advanced Security Features
articles.summary: New STM32U5 microcontroller series combines ultra-low power consumption with advanced security features including TrustZone and hardware crypto acceleration for IoT applications.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has introduced the STM32U5 series, the company's most advanced ultra-low-power microcontrollers featuring state-of-the-art security capabilities. The new series targets demanding IoT applications requiring both extended battery life and robust security.

The STM32U5 series is built on an enhanced ARM Cortex-M33 core with TrustZone technology, providing hardware isolation between secure and non-secure code execution. Key security features include:

- Hardware-based secure boot with root of trust
- AES-256 hardware cryptographic accelerator
- Public key accelerator (PKA) for RSA and ECC operations
- True random number generator (TRNG)
- Active tamper detection and protection
- Secure debug authentication

'With the STM32U5 series, we're addressing the critical need for security in battery-powered IoT devices,' said Ricardo De Sa Earp, Executive Vice President of Microcontrollers at ST. 'Our customers can now implement enterprise-grade security without compromising on power consumption.'

Power consumption is equally impressive, with the STM32U5 achieving 19uA/MHz in active mode and 2.5uA in low-power mode with SRAM retention. The series includes devices with up to 2MB Flash and 786KB SRAM, supporting complex applications with multiple wireless connectivity stacks.

Development support includes the STM32CubeU5 firmware package, Nucleo evaluation boards, and comprehensive security documentation. Mass production is scheduled to begin in Q2 2026.

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Partners with Leading Renewable Energy Company for Next-Generation Solar Inverters
articles.summary: Strategic partnership aims to develop high-efficiency solar inverter solutions using ST's SiC MOSFETs and advanced power management technologies.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has announced a strategic partnership with SolarMax Technologies, a leading renewable energy company, to develop next-generation solar inverter solutions. The collaboration will leverage ST's silicon carbide (SiC) power devices and advanced gate driver technologies to achieve industry-leading efficiency and power density.

The partnership focuses on developing string inverters for residential and commercial solar installations with target efficiency exceeding 99%. By utilizing ST's latest generation SiC MOSFETs, the new inverters will achieve:

- 30% reduction in switching losses compared to silicon IGBT solutions
- 50% smaller heatsink requirements
- 25% reduction in overall system size
- Enhanced reliability with operating temperatures up to 175°C

'ST's SiC technology is a game-changer for solar inverter design,' said Dr. Michael Weber, CTO of SolarMax Technologies. 'The combination of low switching losses and high-temperature capability allows us to push the boundaries of efficiency and reliability.'

The collaboration includes joint development of:
- Optimized power stage designs using ST SiC MOSFETs
- Advanced gate driver solutions with integrated protection
- Digital control algorithms for maximum power point tracking
- Thermal management solutions for high-power density

First products from the partnership are expected to reach the market in Q3 2026, with volume production ramping in 2027.

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Introduces AEC-Q100 Qualified MEMS Sensors for Automotive Applications
articles.summary: New automotive-grade MEMS sensor family includes accelerometers, gyroscopes, and pressure sensors qualified for safety-critical applications including ADAS and vehicle dynamics control.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has expanded its automotive sensor portfolio with a new family of AEC-Q100 qualified MEMS devices designed for safety-critical applications. The new sensors meet the stringent requirements of modern automotive systems including ADAS, vehicle dynamics control, and autonomous driving.

The new automotive MEMS family includes:

**ASM330LHHX - 6-Axis Inertial Module**
- 3-axis accelerometer and 3-axis gyroscope
- AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified (-40°C to +125°C)
- Dual-core architecture for system redundancy
- Suitable for ASIL-B applications

**LPS27AHW - Absolute Pressure Sensor**
- 260-1260 hPa measurement range
- AEC-Q100 Grade 0 qualified (-40°C to +150°C)
- Embedded temperature compensation
- Designed for TPMS and engine control

**AIS2DW12 - Ultra-Low-Power Accelerometer**
- ±2g/±4g/±8g/±16g selectable ranges
- 14-bit resolution with embedded FIFO
- AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified
- Ideal for telematics and theft detection

'Automotive applications demand the highest levels of quality and reliability,' said Simone Ferri, Vice President of MEMS Product Division at ST. 'Our new sensors undergo extensive qualification testing including accelerated life testing, thermal cycling, and mechanical stress testing.'

All devices feature enhanced diagnostic capabilities required for functional safety applications, including continuous self-test, communication error detection, and supply voltage monitoring.

The sensors are supported by dedicated automotive firmware libraries and are compatible with ST's SPC5 automotive microcontroller family. Samples are available now with production ramping in Q2 2026.

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Acquires Edge AI Startup to Strengthen Machine Learning Capabilities
articles.summary: Acquisition of NeuralEdge Technologies brings advanced neural network optimization technology and AI expertise to enhance ST's embedded AI solutions.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has completed the acquisition of NeuralEdge Technologies, a Silicon Valley-based startup specializing in edge AI and neural network optimization. The acquisition strengthens ST's capabilities in embedded machine learning solutions for industrial and consumer applications.

NeuralEdge Technologies has developed proprietary algorithms for optimizing deep neural networks to run efficiently on resource-constrained microcontrollers. Their technology enables complex AI workloads to execute on devices with limited memory and processing power.

'The acquisition of NeuralEdge accelerates our strategy to bring AI to the edge,' said Philippe Magarshack, Executive Vice President of Strategy and Technology at ST. 'Their optimization technology combined with our STM32 ecosystem will enable developers to deploy sophisticated AI models on mainstream microcontrollers.'

Key technologies acquired include:

- Neural network compression and quantization tools
- Automated model optimization for ARM Cortex-M processors
- Specialized AI accelerators for computer vision applications
- Development tools for edge AI deployment

The NeuralEdge team will be integrated into ST's Microcontrollers Division, with the former CEO Dr. Sarah Chen joining as Vice President of Edge AI. The acquisition includes approximately 50 engineers and data scientists with expertise in machine learning and embedded systems.

ST plans to integrate NeuralEdge's technology into the STM32Cube.AI ecosystem, enabling developers to convert pre-trained neural networks into optimized C code for STM32 microcontrollers. The enhanced tools will support popular frameworks including TensorFlow Lite, ONNX, and PyTorch.

Financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Receives Industry Sustainability Award for Carbon Neutrality Progress
articles.summary: ST recognized for achieving 50% reduction in carbon emissions and commitment to carbon neutrality by 2027, leading the semiconductor industry in sustainable manufacturing.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has been honored with the 2026 Semiconductor Sustainability Excellence Award by the Global Semiconductor Alliance (GSA) for its outstanding achievements in environmental sustainability and commitment to carbon neutrality.

The award recognizes ST's progress toward its goal of becoming carbon neutral by 2027, including:

- 50% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions since 2018
- 100% renewable electricity sourcing at all European and Singapore facilities
- Implementation of energy efficiency programs reducing consumption by 25%
- Zero waste to landfill achievement at 15 manufacturing sites
- Water recycling programs saving 2.5 billion liters annually

'Sustainability is core to our business strategy,' said Jean-Marc Chery, President and CEO of STMicroelectronics. 'We believe that environmental responsibility and business success go hand in hand. This award recognizes the dedication of our 50,000 employees worldwide who are committed to making sustainability a reality.'

ST's sustainability initiatives extend beyond manufacturing to product design. The company's PowerGaN and SiC devices enable customers to reduce energy consumption in their end applications, with an estimated 150 million tons of CO2 emissions avoided annually through ST-enabled efficiency improvements.

The company has also implemented a comprehensive sustainable packaging program, eliminating single-use plastics and increasing recycled content in shipping materials by 80%.

'ST is setting the standard for sustainability in the semiconductor industry,' said Jodi Shelton, CEO of GSA. 'Their comprehensive approach to environmental responsibility serves as a model for the entire industry.'

ST will showcase its sustainability initiatives at the upcoming SEMICON West 2026 conference in San Francisco.

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Breaks Ground on New Factory in Morocco
articles.summary: New $300 million facility in Bouskoura will focus on back-end manufacturing and testing, creating 1,200 jobs and strengthening ST's supply chain resilience.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has broken ground on a new manufacturing facility in Bouskoura, Morocco, representing an investment of $300 million. The new factory will focus on back-end manufacturing operations including packaging and testing of semiconductor devices.

The 45,000 square meter facility will employ approximately 1,200 people when fully operational and will serve as a key hub for ST's automotive and industrial product lines. Construction is expected to be completed by Q2 2027, with production ramp beginning in Q4 2027.

'This investment reflects our commitment to building a resilient and diversified supply chain,' said Marco Monti, President of Automotive and Discrete Group at ST. 'Morocco offers an excellent combination of skilled workforce, strategic location, and supportive business environment.'

The new facility will feature:

- Advanced packaging lines for power discretes and analog ICs
- Automated testing equipment for high-volume production
- Clean room facilities for sensitive semiconductor processes
- On-site quality assurance and reliability testing laboratories
- Employee training and development center

The Morocco facility will complement ST's existing manufacturing network, which includes front-end fabs in France, Italy, and Singapore, and back-end facilities in China, Malaysia, and the Philippines.

The project has received support from the Moroccan government through investment incentives and infrastructure development. ST has committed to training programs that will develop local engineering talent and create long-term career opportunities.

'We are proud to contribute to Morocco's growing technology sector,' said Chery. 'This facility will be a center of excellence for semiconductor manufacturing and a testament to the talent and potential of the Moroccan workforce.'

articles.title: STMicroelectronics Unveils RF Front-End Solutions for 5G Infrastructure
articles.summary: New portfolio of RF power amplifiers, switches, and filters designed for 5G base stations and small cells, supporting both sub-6GHz and mmWave frequency bands.
articles.content:
STMicroelectronics has launched a comprehensive portfolio of RF front-end solutions specifically designed for 5G infrastructure applications. The new products address the growing demand for high-performance RF components in base stations, small cells, and massive MIMO systems.

The 5G RF portfolio includes:

**RF Power Amplifiers**
- STACB5G series: High-efficiency Doherty amplifiers for macro base stations
- Frequency coverage: 3.3-3.8GHz (n77/n78 bands) and 4.4-5.0GHz (n79 band)
- Output power: 10W to 200W with 55% efficiency
- Digital pre-distortion (DPD) compatible

**RF Switches**
- SPDT and SP4T switches for antenna tuning and beam steering
- Ultra-low insertion loss (< 0.3dB at 3.5GHz)
- High isolation (> 30dB)
- Fast switching time (< 1us)

**SAW and BAW Filters**
- Band-pass filters for 5G NR frequency bands
- Low insertion loss and excellent out-of-band rejection
- Compact 1.4mm x 1.1mm packages

'The deployment of 5G networks is accelerating globally, creating strong demand for RF components that deliver high performance and reliability,' said Marcello San Biagio, General Manager of RF and Analog Division at ST. 'Our new portfolio leverages ST's expertise in RF technology and high-volume manufacturing.'

The RF power amplifiers utilize ST's advanced LDMOS and GaN-on-SiC technologies, offering industry-leading efficiency and thermal performance. The devices are qualified for outdoor base station applications with operating temperature ranges from -40°C to +85°C.

Development support includes reference designs for common 5G transmitter architectures, simulation models for system design, and application notes for thermal management and matching network design.

The products are sampling now with production volumes available in Q2 2026. ST plans to expand the portfolio to include mmWave solutions for 5G FR2 bands later this year.
